item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have a problem with the surveillance log. I'm not downvoting because I like everything up to that part, but I can't upvote either because of it. It just seems tacked on with no purpose and this part:

No further activity detected. Arm presumed to have dematerialised.

is just plain silly. There were security teams *right there* firing weapons at it, but then suddenly lose sight of it, or what? Not enough development of that part.

It happened! It did the thing! I'd like to thank the Academy, my parents, and my dog.

century club, woooo

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, except whatever that thing with the arm was, it was able to make a new text file appear on a USB drive out of thin air, so whatever's going on, it's still out there.

item.title: Picture ID
item.content:encoded: For the record, the picture illustrating this SCP is https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Ceramic_Abutment_Crown.jpg , taken by Robert Gougaloff, who released it into the public domain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like this should be classed as neutralized. Either way, I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You see its mouth. You hear its eyes. You smell its ear. You taste its nose.

This is the Buried Giant with more cowbell.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I blame Josh for this. (SCP-2712). Maybe this is where he escaped to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the shit that I like, crossing between biology and technology. Love it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2211-2 is a 2.0 MB audio file titled eyee(79).wav. Its contents vary with each playthrough, but generally consist of modulated static that cuts off occasionally every 2-10 seconds for approximately 0.3 seconds.

In case you were wondering, the static cuts off when the eye blinks.

item.title: THX
item.content:encoded: 耳 translates to EAR in Chinese. Well yes we can all use Google translate, but a footnote would be better. Thanks for putting this in my country, gr8 b8 m8 i r8 8/8 dnt h8.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why yes, yes I am.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The best articles aren't necessarily the ones that are the scariest, or the most well-detailed, or those with the longest background. To me, they're the ones where the object being written about makes a certain amount of sense from a perspective that is totally alien to our own. Something that straddles the line between "painfully explained and spelled out" and "weird for the sake of weird so wacky lol".

There is obviously something going on with the stuff in this one, that's not really graspable from a normal perspective. Whatever is going on, however, is apparent enough to be keeping some researchers awake at night. Nicely done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find the "longsmile" reference so creepily apt that I'd like to suggest changing the article title on the index page to just "Longsmile". Great job :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy shit Mac you are a genius

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fantastic. Creepy, bizarre, with just a taste of "Foundation" humor. An instant classic in my opinion. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: longsmile … <shudder> +1 for you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was pleasantly bizarre to read. +1

item.title: Hmmmm.
item.content:encoded: Did no one think to try playing all of them at the same time? I mean… who knows what'd happen

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love longsmile, that's a great image. The rest is at least mildly interesting, and I definitely like the 1193-esque arm. That whole incident log is great. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Haha, 1193 was kind of an inspiration for this one! It's one of my all time faves on the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My thoughts exactly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is totes the cousin SCP-1193 was talking about.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of the most enjoyable articles I've read in a while! It's great to see an old author triumphantly return to the site. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You managed to make some very small anomalies seem very menacing and bizarre. Kudos for that. +1.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Author, please don't add your own tags if you don't fully grasp the tag systen. In this case you made the basic error of not adding a class tag. Please wait for one of my minions esteemed colleagues to add them for you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ugh Ugh Ugh nothign freaks me out more than anything with teeth. The fact that you actually scared me secures my upvote. This is just frikkin' weird and creepy and I like it and I'm never ever going to read it again because ugh ugh ugh teeeeth

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think he self-deleted for quality control reasons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, didn't Kaktus have an SCP in this slot?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed this one quite a lot. Bizarre and surreal imagery, with enough information to piece together what is happening, but not enough to answer every question, allowing me to form my own theories. In short, my favorite type of skip. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmmm self-insert at the end….

When run on a computer, SCP-2211-3 uses a significant amount of memory, resulting in overheating and causing the built-in fans of the affected computer to speed up.

What happens in SSD's or other units that don't generally have fans?

Dunno. Clinically good but it don't grab me. Which is fine, you can't please everyone, nor should you try to.

No vote, but I think it'll do just fine anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It depends on which kind of Foundation you have in your head, the genre savvy one or the by-the-book one. This article's take is that "the big scary monster formed outta nowhere like a bioelectronic megazord, walked out the door and disappeared" and "even if it's extradimensional there's still a chance it will come back".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Class: Safe Uncontained

Object Class, and I imagine this would be Keter rather than just having no class at all. actually, if there's no more anomalous properties, wouldn't this be neutralized? And wouldn't this still be Safe (maaaybe Euclid) even if there were anomalous properties but it just went missing?
